div.head: 概覽
div.p: 最西邊的29號刻經窟經過精心設計。 全窟所刻出自同一部經，即十二卷本《佛說佛名經》前四卷。 共羅列三千八百個佛名，皆冠以“南无”二字（意為禮拜）， 直接從方盒形窟內的入口處開始刊刻，可惜開頭的幾行已不存。刻工對能夠使用的石壁表面仔細規劃。 刻經區域與壁面空間的配合嚴絲合縫。在第一個壁面上，經文一直延伸到窟頂下4釐米和地面上11釐米處。 要在那麽低的地方刻字一定十分困難。可以想像刻工如何躺在地上努力控制著刻刀， 尤其是在d壁前，那裡的刻經區域較其他兩個壁面更加接近地面。
刻工每刻完一百個佛名，通常會用兩個或四個小字註明“某千某百”，而在第一卷末尾有確切的數字“八百卌五”。 在三面牆上迄今總共存留有二十二個這樣的明確記號。最後一個記號距離入口36釐米， 註明刻完了三千八百個佛名又在後面加了一個“竟”（意為“完成”）字。很可能另有三處有此語法。見本卷第頁和第頁，“佛經標識”。我們彷彿聼到刻工長呼出一口氣，如釋重負。

div.head: 位置

list.item: 該窟位於南崖A區，窟底在現今路面上方約107釐米處。
f壁方向：338°。

div.p: 29號窟西側有寬25釐米的崖壁，將其與28號龕隔開，與東側的30號坐佛像之間又有寬17釐米的崖壁（i壁）相隔。

figure.head: 29號窟平面圖；雷魯卡繪製
Ground plan of cave 29; drawing Lukas Ledderose

div.head: 形制
div.p: 敞口方形窟，帶近代門檻，門檻高14至18釐米，寬30釐米。窟室平面近方形，前窄後寬。地面前部平坦，後部高低不平，e壁前的地面有一個不規則的隆起。窟頂大體水平，由外向內略往上擡升，並伸出窟外約18釐米。
門檻外的入口區域平坦，進深約100至130釐米。這一區域延續至A區的中央壁龕（31號龕）。d壁的底部與窟外突出的岩石融為一體。

list.item: d壁：前部高232釐米，後部高238釐米，寬248釐米。
e壁：西端高244釐米，中央高250釐米，東端高243釐米，寬249釐米。
f壁：前部高226釐米，後部高242釐米，寬228釐米。
窟口寬207釐米。
入口附近有人為開鑿的孔洞，呈縱向排列，d壁六個，f壁十四個，曾用來安裝門框。現在一個鐵柵門被固定在窟口的西側，東側可以打開。2011年修建了一個可覆蓋A區所有洞窟的長簷。

figure.head: A 區29號窟；2005年拍攝
Cave 29 in section A; photograph taken in 2005

list.item: 打磨面一直延伸到窟頂，結束於距地面10至16釐米、距e壁10至15釐米處。
刻字區原為一個高216釐米、寬193釐米的縱長方形。上緣距窟頂4釐米（入口處）至16釐米（後部），下緣距地面19釐米（入口處）至11釐米（後部），距e牆21至23釐米。
前四行已完全磨滅，第五行“淨眼”二字仍見於拓片上（見），窟壁上第七行可見“佛南无”三字。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 壁面前部已毀損。在門檻上方約152釐米處，一個三角形凹陷切入原來的刻字區約5釐米。距地面85釐米處，一條水平裂縫橫穿整個壁面。自壁面中部的窟頂下方，一條裂縫向入口處蜿蜒斜伸，直至門檻上方約87釐米處。在兩邊的潮濕部分，許多字符已不存。

row.cell: ［佛說佛名經卷第一］；已佚，根據d壁第70行復原而成；標識《佛說佛名經》第一卷開始。
八百卌五；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識第一卷刻了845個佛名。未標出第一卷卷末。
佛說佛名經卷第二；其後有“一切經”三字；標識《佛說佛名經》第二卷開始。
一千；雙行夾註，合計二字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1000個佛名。
一千一百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1100個佛名。

figure.head: 29號窟刻文區概覽，自右往左依次為d壁、e壁、f和i壁
Overview of the fields of script in cave 29, on walls d, e, f, and i, from right to left

list.item: 整個壁面經過打磨，直至不規則的石窟底部。
刻字區為一個高200釐米、寬220釐米的縱長方形。距d壁12至15釐米，上緣距窟頂13至26釐米，下緣距窟地面20至28釐米，距f壁13至17釐米。

row.cell: 上部中間可見橫向線條

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 表面平整，大部分已磨蝕，尤其是下部。地面上方約170釐米處有幾條細窄的水平裂紋，在與f壁相交處逐漸擴大。

row.cell: 一千二百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1200個佛名。
一千四百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1400個佛名。
一千五百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1500個佛名。
［佛］名經卷第二；標識《佛說佛名經》第二卷結束。
□□一千□□□□□，字徑等同正文；根據第56行“一千五百”佛名數至此實際數出的佛名，以及f壁第87行第四卷卷末的用語，或可復原為“［右件］一千［五百九十竟］”。詳情見隸定錄文。
佛說佛名經卷第三；標識《佛說佛名經》第三卷開始。
［一千七百］；已佚，雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；根據第82行和第89行復原而成；標識至此刻了1700個佛名。從第三卷卷端至此，正好有100個佛名，有一個空格可刻佛名數。參考第89行的佛名數可知，刻工認為第二卷結束處已刻1600個佛名。
一千［八］百；一字佚失，雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；根據第75行和第89行復原而成；標識至此刻了1800個佛名。
一千九百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了1900個佛名。
二千；雙行夾註，合計二字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2000個佛名。
二千一百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2100個佛名。

list.item: 打磨面從地面上方約23至26釐米、距e壁約12釐米處開始，一直延伸至入口和窟頂；但在上部靠近e壁的角落還留有溝痕。
原本的刻字區為一個高187釐米、寬178釐米的縱長方形。下緣距窟地面28釐米（後部），近窟口處距地面24釐米，距入口36釐米，距後壁9至16釐米，上緣距窟頂13至33釐米（後部）。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 表面平整。大部分已磨蝕，尤其是下部。地面上方約102釐米處有一條細窄的水平裂紋。

row.cell: 二千二百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2200個佛名。
二千三百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2300個佛名。
二千四百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2400個佛名。
二千五百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2500個佛名。
二千六百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了2600個佛名。
佛說佛名經卷第三；標識《佛說佛名經》第三卷結束。
佛說佛名經卷第四；標識《佛說佛名經》第四卷開始。
三［千］竟；雙行夾註，合計三字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了3000個佛名。
三千一百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了3100個佛名。
右件［三千二百竟］；字徑等同正文；根據第51行“三千一百”佛名數至此實際數出的佛名，以及f壁第87行第四卷卷末的用語復原而成；標識至此刻了3200個佛名。
三千六百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了3600個佛名。
三千七百；雙行夾註，合計四字字徑等同正文一字；標識至此刻了3700個佛名。
右件三千八百竟；字徑等同正文；標識至此終止處刻了3800個佛名。
佛說佛名經卷第四；標識《佛說佛名經》第四卷結束。

list.item: 刊刻於洞窟外的i壁。打磨面大小為高118釐米、寬17釐米，刻字區大小為高87釐米、寬14釐米。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 原本刊刻較淺，但刻字大多可以辨認。

row.cell: 般若波羅蜜多心經；標識《般若波羅蜜多心經》開始。
般若波羅蜜多心經；標識《般若波羅蜜多心經》結束。

ab.abbr: 雷德侯 任婧 蔡穗玲

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
胡文和、李官智第20頁
胡文和和李官智確認該窟兩部佛經的經題為：《佛名經》和《般若波羅蜜多心經》。

彭家勝第6頁
彭家勝提供了該窟高、寬、深三維數據，進而計算29號窟體積為11.55立方米。他判定該窟刻經為《佛名經》，並指出卷二處於d壁，卷四位於f壁。他還辨認出i壁所刻為《般若波羅蜜多心經》。他稱各壁保存現狀“完好”。

曹丹第51頁
曹丹確定d壁、e壁和f壁所刻是一部完整的經本，即《佛名經》卷一至四，共列出了3800多個佛名。他計算出該窟刻經面面積為12.9846平方米。

胡文和第8頁
胡文和記錄了該窟的三維數據（同彭家勝第6頁），不過並未計算其體積。他採用了彭家勝（）對各壁所刻經文的看法。

李良、鄧之金 第39頁
李良和鄧之金認為，該窟開鑿於唐代。他們描述了該窟形制，並測量了尺寸，還測量了刻經區的尺寸並對所刻經文做了如下判斷：
d壁：高217 釐米、寬185釐米，刻有《佛名經》，計85行，每行98字，共8330字。
e壁：高205釐米、寬225釐米，刻有《佛說佛名經卷第四》，計109行，每行101字，共11009字。
f壁：高190釐米、寬178釐米，刻有《佛說佛名經卷第四》，計87行，每行101字，共8787字。
f壁：高89釐米、寬18釐米，刻有《般若波羅蜜多心經》，計7行，每行42字，共294字。
他們將該窟的整體保存狀況描述為“ 基本完好”。

成都考古發現第361－362頁，第404頁
這份報告介紹了洞窟的位置、形制、尺寸和保存狀況，提到了一些自然形成的裂縫和正立面上後來的人為添加物。
該報告確定所刻佛經為菩提流支譯《佛名經》，並詳盡描述各壁刻經的保存狀況。該報告作者考察了經文布局，認為刻經不完整。由於i壁玄奘譯本《心經》與《佛名經》字體不同，因而認為《心經》刊刻較晚。兩部經的內容也並不協調。
儘管洞窟原來的地面已經大面積風蝕，後壁e底部還有部分存留。該報告提到d壁刻字區下緣在原本的地面之下，表明經文並非在洞窟開鑿之後立即刻就，而是在刻經時地面只挖了一部分。與d壁不同的是，e壁和f壁的刻經面下緣位於原來的地面之上。

曹丹、于春 11
曹丹和于春提供石壁的尺寸和表面積，並根據拓片數出所存刻經的字數：d壁9134字，e壁10573字，f壁7740字。兩位學者辨識出《佛名經》的四卷佛經標識，並提及卷四末的佛名數“三千八百”。

李琛妍第47－48頁，第68頁
李琛妍發表了一張29號窟正立面和30號至32號龕的照片（第47頁，圖12）。她認為，這些窟龕都開鑿於8世紀上半葉。
她指出，臥佛的頭部與洞窟中心垂直於27號至38號龕（該文稱作第二組）。在她看來，這樣的安排形成一種動態的空間連續，使得巨型佛像與第二組造像在主題上相聯。並非巧合的是，後者通過三佛的母題（31號龕及其兩邊兩尊大型坐佛像）和《佛名經》，讚頌佛陀在宇宙間無限而連續的存在。該文作者認為，這一主題上的關聯也見於南崖其他刻經窟組。

div.head: Overview
div.p: The westernmost sutra cave 29 was extremely well planned. In this instance, parts of only one sutra, the first four of the twelve scrolls of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas , fill the cave completely. The list with the names of the 3,800 Buddhas, every single one of them preceded by namo 南无 (pay homage to), started immediately at the entrance of the box-shaped interior, but the first columns have vanished. The carvers used the available surface with circumspection. The fields of script fit tightly into the wall spaces. On the first wall, the text reaches up to 4 cm below the ceiling and down to 11 cm above the floor. It must have been very difficult to carve characters at such a low level. One imagines the carver lying on the floor while grappling with his chisel, especially in front of wall d, where the field of script continues further down than on the other two walls.

Once they had finished cutting one hundred names, the carvers often noted “so and so many hundred” in two or four small characters; at the end of scroll 1, the precise number “845” is given. In total, 22 such markers are still present on the three walls. The last marker, a comfortable 36 cm from the entrance, records that 3,800 names had been completed. One additional character jing 竟, completion, is appended here, and possibly in three other places.See below, p. and p. , Text markers. We can almost hear the carver’s sigh of relief.

figure.head: 29號窟平面圖；雷魯卡繪製
Ground plan of cave 29; drawing Lukas Ledderose

div.head: Location

list.item: Southern slope of the valley, area A, ca. 107 cm above present walkway.
Direction: wall f 338˚.

div.ab: Separated on the west by the cliff wall, width 25 cm, from the small shrine 28, and, on the east, by the cliff wall (wall i), width 17 cm, from the seated Buddha figure 30.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box shape, open front with recent threshold, which is 14–18 cm high and 30 cm wide. The ground plan is approximately square, but widens toward the rear; the floor is flat in the front, but uneven at the rear; there is an irregular elevation at the foot of wall e; the horizontal ceiling rises slightly toward the rear, and at the front extends out beyond the cave by ca. 18 cm.

figure.head: A 區29號窟；2007年拍攝
Cave 29 in Section A; photograph taken in 2007

div.p: A flat entrance area extends ca. 100 to 130 cm outside the threshold. It continues toward the central niche of section A (niche 31). The lower part of wall d merges into the bulging rock outside.

list.item: Wall d: height at the front 232 cm, at the rear 238 cm, width 248 cm.
Wall e: height at western corner 244 cm, at midpoint 250 cm, at eastern corner 243 cm, width 249 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 226 cm, at the rear 242 cm, width 228 cm.
Width of opening: 207 cm.

Vertical rows of man-made holes near the entrance, 6 in wall d and 14 in wall f, formerly used for a door frame. Presently, the western half of a steel grill is fixed, but the eastern half can be opened. A long roof built in 2011 extends over the caves and niches of section A (fig. ).
The polished area extends all the way from the ceiling; it ends 10–16 cm above the floor, and 10–15 cm from wall e.

The original field of script was a vertical rectangle, 216 x 193 cm. The distance from the ceiling varies from 4 cm at the opening to 16 cm at the rear of the cave; the distance between the lowest row and the floor varies from 19 cm at the opening to 11 cm at the rear of the cave; the distance from the rear wall is 21–23 cm.

The first 4 columns are missing. The two characters jing yan 淨眼 in column 5 are preserved in the rubbing (see plate ). In column 7 three characters fo namo 佛南无 of the text are still visible on the wall.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: The front part of the wall is damaged. At a height of ca. 152 cm above the threshold, a triangular indentation of the wall cuts about 5 cm into the original field of script. A horizontal crack runs through the entire wall at a height of ca. 85 cm above the floor. A diagonal crack starts beneath the ceiling in the middle of the wall and undulates diagonally toward the entrance, ending ca. 87 cm above the threshold. In the moist areas at its two sides many characters have been lost.

row.cell: ［佛說佛名經卷第一］; not visible, by analogy with wall d, column 70; refers to the beginning of scroll 1, of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas (Fo shuo foming jing 佛說佛名經).

八百卌五; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 845 carved Buddha names from the first scroll. The end of scroll 1 is not marked, perhaps due to lack of space.
佛說佛名經卷第二; refers to the beginning of scroll 2 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas ; followed by the three characters yiqie jing 一切經, all sutras.
一千一百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 1,100 Buddha names.

list.item: The polished area covers the entire wall down to the irregular rocky foot.

Field of script, vertical rectangle 200 x 220 cm; the distance from wall d varies from 12–15 cm; the distance from the ceiling varies from 13–26 cm; the distance of the lowest row from the floor varies from 20–28 cm; the distance from wall f varies from 13–17 cm.

row.cell: horizontal line visible in upper middle part
vertical lines visible in upper part

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: The surface is even; large areas are worn, especially in the lower part. Several thin horizontal cracks, ca. 170 cm above the floor, widen at the edge with wall f.

row.cell: 意住持佛……; continuation from wall d, column 89.
一千二百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 1,200 Buddha names.
一千四百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 1,400 Buddha names.
一千五百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 1,500 Buddha names.
［佛］名經卷第二; refers to the end of scroll 2 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas .

□□一千□□□□□; standard size characters arranged in a column; possible reconstruction “［右件］一千［五百九十竟］” based on counting the names from the number 1,500 in column 56 and by analogy with wall f, column 87, the end of scroll 4; refers to the completion of 1,590 Buddha names to the right.For more detail see the transcription on .
佛說佛名經卷第三; refers to the beginning of scroll 3 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas .

［一千七百］; not visible; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; reconstruction of the number by analogy with columns 82 and 89; refers to the completion of 1,700 Buddha names. From the beginning of scroll 3 to this column 75, where there is a space for a number, there are exactly 100 Buddha names. By reference to the number in column 89 it is inferred that the carvers believed that scroll 2 ended with 1,600 Buddha names.

一千［八］百; one character missing; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; reconstruction of the number by analogy with columns 75 and 89; refers to the completion of 1,800 Buddha names.
一千九百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 1,900 Buddha names.
二千一百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,100 Buddha names.

list.item: The polished area begins ca. 12 cm from wall e, and extends all the way to the entrance and to the ceiling; it ends 23–26 cm above the floor; in the upper corner near wall e striations remain.

The original field of script was a vertical rectangle, 187 x 178 cm; the distance from the rear wall varies from 9–16 cm; the distance from the ceiling varies from 13–33 cm at the rear; the distance of the lowest row from the floor is 28 cm at the rear and 24 cm further toward the entrance; the distance from the entrance is 36 cm.

row.cell: vertical lines visible in upper part

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: The surface is even; large areas are worn, especially in the lower part. A thin horizontal crack extends ca. 102 cm above the floor.

row.cell: 南无師子佛……; continuation from column 109 of wall e.
二千二百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,200 Buddha names.
二千三百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,300 Buddha names.
二千四百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,400 Buddha names.
二千五百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,500 Buddha names.
二千六百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 2,600 Buddha names.
佛說佛名經卷第三; refers to the end of scroll 3 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas .
佛說佛名經卷第四; refers to the beginning of scroll 4 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas .
三［千］竟; three small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 3,000 Buddha names.
三千一百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 3,100 Buddha names.
右件［三千二百竟］; standard size characters arranged in a column; reconstruction of the number by analogy with wall f, columns 87; refers to the completion of 3,200 Buddha names to the right.
三千六百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 3,600 Buddha names.
三千七百; four small characters arranged in a square, the size of one large character; refers to the completion of 3,700 Buddha names.
右件三千八百竟; standard size characters arranged in a column; refers to the completion of 3,800 Buddha names to the right.
佛說佛名經卷第四; refers to the end of scroll 4 of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Names of the Buddhas .

list.item: Engraved on the outside of the cave, wall i.
Polished area: 118 x 17 cm.
Field of script: 87 x 14 cm.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Originally rather shallow carving, but most characters are recognizable.

row.cell: 般若波羅蜜多心經; marks the beginning of the Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutra (Bore boluomiduo xinjing 般若波羅蜜多心經).
般若波羅蜜多心經; marks the end of the Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutra .

note.p:
Hu and Li identify the titles of the two sutras at the cave as:
Sutra on the Names of the Buddhas
佛名經 and the
Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutra
般若波羅蜜多心經.

Peng gives measurements of the dimensions of the three walls of the cave and computes its volume as 11.55 cubic meters. He identifies the Sutra on the Names of the Buddhas
佛名經 and locates scroll 2 of the sutra on wall d and scroll 4 on wall f. The
Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutra
般若波羅蜜多心經 he identifies on wall i. He describes the preservation of the walls as “very good 完好”.

Cao establishes that on walls d, e, and f, scrolls 1–4 of the
Sutra on the Names of the Buddhas
佛名經 were carved in their entirety, presenting more than 3,800 Buddha names. He gives the wall surface of engraved texts in this cave as 12.9846 square meters.
Hu sets out the measurements of the three dimensions of the cave (as does 6), but he does not compute its volume. Hu adopts the same locations of the texts as given by .

Li and Deng believe that this cave was carved in the Tang dynasty. They describe the shape of the cave and give its measurements. They also give measurements of the fields of script and identify the sutra as follows:

list.item:
wall d: 217 x 185 cm, carved with the Sutra on the Buddha Names
佛名經, in 85 columns, with 98 characters in each column, 8,330 in total.

wall e: 205 x 225 cm; carved with the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Buddha Names
佛說佛名經, scroll 4, in 109 columns, with 101 characters in each column, 11,009 in total.

wall f: 190 x 178 cm; carved with the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Buddha Names
佛說佛名經, scroll 4, in 87 columns, with 101 characters in each column, 8,787 in total.

wall i: 89 x 18 cm; carved with the Heart Sutra
般若波羅蜜多心經 in 7 columns, with 42 characters in each column, 294 in total.

note.p: They describe the overall state of preservation of this cave as “basically good, 基本完好.”
The authors give location, shape, measurements, and state of preservation of the cave. Mention is made of some natural cracks and later man-made additions to the facade.

The Sutra on the Names of the Buddhas
佛名經 is identified as the version translated by Bodhiruci; a detailed description of the state of preservation of the text on each wall is given. The authors assign each of the four scrolls to the wall or walls where it was carved. The carving is regarded as incomplete. Because the style of characters of the Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutra on wall i, in the translation by Xuanzang, differs from that of the Sutra on the Names of the Buddhas , it is thought to have been engraved at a later date. The contents of the two sutras are also regarded as not complementing one another.

Although the original floor in the cave has largely weathered away, a section still remains at the foot of the rear wall e. The lower edge of the field of script on wall d is described as being below the level of the original floor. It is, therefore, suggested that the sutra was not engraved immediately after the cave was hollowed out, and that, at the time of carving, the floor had only been partly dug out. Unlike wall d, the lower edges of the fields of script on walls e and f are above the original floor.

Cao and Yu give measurements of the walls, their surface areas, and based on rubbings the number of extant characters, wall d: 9,134; wall e: 10,573; wall f: 7,740. They identify the four scroll titles and note the number of 3,800 Buddha names at the end of scroll four.
Lee publishes a photograph of the facade of cave 29 and of the niches 30–32 (p. 47, fig. 12). She suggests that they were all carved in the first half of the eighth century.

The head of the reclining Buddha statue, she points out, is placed perpendicular to the center of caves and niches 27–38 (called cluster II in her paper). In her view, such an arrangement creates a dynamic spatial continuum that thematically connects the colossal Buddha to cluster II. The latter, not coincidentally, celebrates the infinite, continuous succession of Buddhas in the universe through the three Buddha motif (niche 31 and the two large seated Buddha statues on its sides) and the Sutra of the Names of Buddhas . This thematic link, the author claims, is further developed among other clusters along the southern cliff.
